items.title: More than 800 northern corroboree frogs released into the wild in southern NSW to bolster numbers

items.title: Porepunkah property owner says he helped a wounded officer after Dezi Freeman shot at police
items.summary: A Porepunkah property owner says he helped a wounded police officer after the fatal shooting when Dezi Freeman killed two Victoria police officers.

items.title: Emotional Senate tributes for Kumanjayi Little Baby amid debate on focus of NT inquiry
items.summary: Senator Jacinta Nampijinpa Price weeps through an impassioned tribute to her niece, Kumanjayi Little Baby, in federal parliament, amid a push to broaden the focus of an inquiry announced in the wake of her alleged murder.

items.title: Man charged with son's torture and murder previously offered to plead guilty to manslaughter, court hears
items.summary: Reinhardt Bosch has been charged with the murder and torture of his son Rhuan in 2022, and offered to plead guilty to the lesser charge of manslaughter, a court has heard.

items.title: Murderer sentenced to life in prison over death of 22yo Dale Aaron McKenna
items.summary: The court in Toowoomba heard Clinton Gordon Sutcliffe was motivated by revenge after discovering his on-again off-again girlfriend had started a relationship with the 22-year-old victim.

items.title: Sydney cage fighter found not guilty of murder but guilty of manslaughter for killing best friend
items.summary: Bradley Fletcher has been found not guilty of murder but guilty of manslaughter for killing his best friend during a backyard sparring session and leaving his body by a footpath.

items.title: Yindjibarndi people win $150m compensation after Andrew Forrest's Fortescue mines built without permission
items.summary: Andrew Forrest's Fortescue has been ordered to pay $150 million to WA traditional owners after mining their land without permission.

items.title: Queensland government says it will continue with Paradise Dam rebuild despite advice to ditch project
items.summary: The board of Sunwater has written to the state government, labelling the rebuild of Paradise Dam "grossly disproportionate" and should be "no further considered".

items.title: Government accused of 'burying' response to landmark gambling report with budget day release
items.summary: A long-awaited response to an inquiry that called for a gambling advertising ban has been tabled on budget day, prompting accusations the government is trying to bury the document.

items.title: Holmes à Court family-owned Heytesbury buys 11 live export ships in major deal with Vroon
items.summary: Owned by the Holmes à Court family, Heytesbury runs 165,000 head of cattle in northern Australia and has just struck a deal with Vroon to buy its ships.

items.title: Murdoch University promises improvements after viral video shows issues with wheelchair access

items.title: Jack Alex Cullen jailed for trafficking cocaine while in Australian Defence Force

items.title: Multiple sclerosis patient spends night at Adelaide hospital waiting for an access taxi

items.title: Coach Michael Voss resigns from AFL strugglers Carlton, Mick Malthouse says premiership winning player will be relieved

items.title: Bullsbrook Centre for National Resilience to be used to quarantine passengers from hantavirus ship
items.summary: Nestled away in Perth's outer fringes this 500-bed facility was built during the COVID-19 pandemic as a quarantine centre, but sat idle for years. Now, a different deadly virus will see it used for its intended purpose.

items.title: Health minister invited to spend week with regional NDIS family

items.title: Alexei Popyrin brushed aside by world number one Jannik Sinner at Italian Open
items.summary: Alexei Popyrin has been outclassed in Rome, beaten 6-2 6-0 by world number one Jannik Sinner in 65 one-sided minutes in the Italian Open third round.

items.title: Homes Tasmania might come and go before Felicity gets a rental
items.summary: Felicity Hirst says she is not too picky about what kind of house she lives in; she just wants one. She has been on Tasmania's social housing list for three and a half years — as long as the state's housing authority has existed.

items.title: The caring burden for single, childless women

items.summary: Australian citizens Kira and Igor Korolev were charged in 2024, accused of attempting to gather and send state secrets to Russia.

items.summary: An Aboriginal man has pleaded guilty to vandalising a culturally significant site in Victoria's Alpine National Park.
